Class SyntacticalReplaceVisitor

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.SyntacticalReplaceVisitor
All Implemented Interfaces:
DefaultVisitor, Visitor<Term>
Direct Known Subclasses:
ConstraintAwareSyntacticalReplaceVisitor
public class SyntacticalReplaceVisitor extends Object implements DefaultVisitor
visitor for method Term.execPostOrder(Visitor). Called with that method on a term, the visitor builds a new term replacing SchemaVariables with their instantiations that are given as a SVInstantiations object.

    SyntacticalReplaceVisitor(TermLabelState termLabelState, SVInstantiations svInst, Services services)

    ONLY TO BE USED BY ConstraintAwareSyntacticalReplaceVisitor (HACK) constructs a term visitor replacing any occurrence of a schemavariable found in svInst by its instantiation

  • Constructor Details

    • SyntacticalReplaceVisitor

      public SyntacticalReplaceVisitor(TermLabelState termLabelState, Taclet.TacletLabelHint labelHint, PosInOccurrence applicationPosInOccurrence, Services services, Rule rule, RuleApp ruleApp, boolean useTermCache)
      ONLY TO BE USED BY ConstraintAwareSyntacticalReplaceVisitor (HACK) constructs a term visitor replacing any occurrence of a schemavariable found in svInst by its instantiation
      Parameters:
      termLabelState - the termlabel state
      labelHint - hints about how to deal with labels
      applicationPosInOccurrence - the application position
      services - the services
      rule - the applied rule
      ruleApp - the rule application
      useTermCache - the TermBuilder to use (allows to use the non cached version)

    • SyntacticalReplaceVisitor

      public SyntacticalReplaceVisitor(TermLabelState termLabelState, Taclet.TacletLabelHint labelHint, PosInOccurrence applicationPosInOccurrence, SVInstantiations svInst, Goal goal, Rule rule, RuleApp ruleApp)
      constructs a term visitor replacing any occurrence of a schemavariable found in svInst by its instantiation
      Parameters:
      termLabelState - the termlabel state
      labelHint - hints about how to deal with labels
      applicationPosInOccurrence - the application position
      svInst - mapping of schemavariables to their instantiation
      goal - the current goal
      rule - the applied rule
      ruleApp - the rule application

    • SyntacticalReplaceVisitor

      public SyntacticalReplaceVisitor(TermLabelState termLabelState, SVInstantiations svInst, Services services)
      ONLY used by TriggeredInstantiations
      Parameters:
      termLabelState - the termlabel state
      svInst - mapping of schemavariables to their instantiation
      services - the Services with information about the logic signature, access to term construction etc.

      protected <T extends Term> T toTerm(T o)
      the method is only still invoked to allow the ConstraintAwareSyntacticalReplaceVisitor to recursively replace meta variables

    • visit

      public void visit(Term p_visited)
      performs the syntactic replacement of schemavariables with their instantiations

      Parameters:
      p_visited - the Term to be visited

    • getTerm

      public JTerm getTerm()
      delivers the new built term

    • getSVInstantiations

      public SVInstantiations getSVInstantiations()

    • subtreeEntered

      public void subtreeEntered(Term subtreeRoot)

      this method is called in execPreOrder and execPostOrder in class Term when entering the subtree rooted in the term subtreeRoot. Default implementation is to do nothing. Subclasses can override this method when the visitor behaviour depends on informations bound to subtrees.

      By default, it does nothing

      Specified by:
      subtreeEntered in interface Visitor<Term>
      Parameters:
      subtreeRoot - root of the subtree which the visitor enters.

    • subtreeLeft

      public void subtreeLeft(Term subtreeRoot)
      this method is called in execPreOrder and execPostOrder in class Term when leaving the subtree rooted in the term subtreeRoot. Default implementation is to do nothing. Subclasses can override this method when the visitor behaviour depends on information bound to subtrees.
      Specified by:
      subtreeLeft in interface Visitor<Term>
      Parameters:
      subtreeRoot - root of the subtree which the visitor leaves.
